title: Minimalist Plywood Pedestal Table Base
summary: A four-leg plywood pedestal built from ripped-and-joined veneer strips — a different construction from this catalog's boxy 2x6 round-table base, meant for a separately-sourced round or square top.
description:
This base is built from four L-shaped leg sets, each one two ripped plywood strips joined at 90° with pocket holes — no 2x lumber anywhere, so the whole base reads as a single clean minimalist column rather than a framed box. Rip a full sheet of veneer plywood into two 16"-wide strips and one 15-1/4"-wide strip, then rip a fourth 15-1/4"-wide strip from one of the 16"-wide pieces, before crosscutting everything into the eight 28-1/2"-long leg pieces.

Drill pocket holes along one edge of each 15-1/4"-wide piece and join it to a 16"-wide piece to build four identical leg sets. **Join the four leg sets together with 1x4 end caps at the top** rather than a solid plywood platform — this is what keeps the base light while still giving every leg set a shared connection point. Reinforce every joint with scrap plywood and brad nails and glue, since a pedestal base carries the full load of the tabletop through just these four corner joints.

**Mending plates on top are the intended tabletop attachment method** — for a top that's only 3/4" thick at the attachment points, scrap plywood blocks can substitute for the plates. This base is designed to carry a separately-built round or square top; it does not include instructions for building the top itself.

tools.note: For ripping the plywood into strips.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut from scrap to reinforce leg-set joints.

steps.title: Rip the plywood into strips
steps.body: Rip the sheet into two 16"-wide strips and one 15-1/4"-wide strip. Rip a fourth 15-1/4"-wide strip from one of the 16"-wide pieces. Crosscut all four strips into 28-1/2" lengths, eight pieces total.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along one edge of each narrow leg piece. Join a narrow piece to a wide piece to build four identical L-shaped leg sets.

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: Join the four leg sets together with the end caps using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Reinforce the joints
steps.body: Reinforce the joints with scrap plywood reinforcement blocks, brad nails, and glue.

steps.title: Add the mending plates
steps.body: Add mending plates to the top to attach your tabletop. For a 3/4"-thick attachment point, scrap plywood blocks can substitute for the plates.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Minimalist plywood pedestal dining table base
